name: barbell front raise and pullover
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a barbell with an overhand grip, palms facing down.
Keep your arms straight and raise the barbell in front of you until it reaches shoulder height.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the barbell back down to the starting position.
Next, lower the barbell behind your head, keeping your arms straight.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then raise the barbell back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a barbell with an overhand grip, palms facing down. Keep your arms straight and raise the barbell in front of you until it reaches shoulder height. Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the barbell back down to the starting position. Next, lower the barbell behind your head, keeping your arms straight. Pause for a moment at the bottom, then raise the barbell back up to the starting position. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

conditionNotes.note: Keep load symmetrical and stop at positions that create sharp pain or twisting.
Use pain-free range of motion, slower tempo, and lighter progressions when joints feel irritated.
Weight-bearing resistance exercises are beneficial for bone density when load is appropriate.
Maintain neutral spine and avoid positions that aggravate the lower back.
Maintain consistent breathing throughout. Avoid breath-holding.
Both aerobic and resistance training improve insulin sensitivity. Monitor blood sugar before, during, and after sessions. Have a fast-acting carb available.
Rhythm-based exercises and large-amplitude movements are beneficial. Focus on motor confidence.
Focus on muscle co-contraction and joint stability. Avoid locking out joints passively.
Functional patterns are generally safe. Prioritize pelvic floor awareness and avoid Valsalva.
Start light with higher reps and shorter ROM. Progress load gradually as pain-free capacity increases.
